name: Barbell Hip Thrust
resistance_profile: Peak glute tension in the shortened (top) position; a strong contraction-biased glute builder.
rom_notes: Lower under control, drive to full hip extension with a hard glute squeeze.
cues: Drive through the heels to full lockout
Squeeze the glutes at the top, no lumbar hyperextension
execution_steps: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench and roll a padded barbell over your hips.
Set your feet flat and shoulder-width so your shins are vertical at the top.
Tuck your chin, brace your core and keep your ribs down.
Drive through your heels to lift the bar until your hips reach full lockout.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top without over-arching your lower back.
Lower under control back toward the floor and repeat.
good_when: You want maximal glute overload with a strong peak contraction.
You want a glute-focused compound that spares the lower back relative to squats.
You are progressing from the glute bridge and want a fuller range of motion.
bad_when: You lack a bench or padded bar and setup is impractical (choose the floor glute bridge).
You want a stretch-biased glute exercise (choose an RDL or deep squat).
common_errors: Overarching the lower back at the top
Short range / not reaching full extension
